query: SELECT id, event_date, title, summary FROM timeline_events ORDER BY event_date ASC;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2N1WTErRdSjheBh.e46440e12186 --database 3yhU3DrKzf8Z5BNtvxatFF --execute SELECT id, event_date, title, summary FROM timeline_events ORDER BY event_date ASC;

rows.title: Declaration of probable cause and arrest
rows.summary: Washoe County Sheriff's Office probable-cause materials identify Cameron Doyle Church as arrestee. Charges: Grand Larceny of a Motor Vehicle and failure-to-register. Confirmed by Master Evidence Index and local 03-12-23 probable-cause PDF.

rows.title: Early case assignment request filed
rows.summary: Washoe County District Attorney's office filed early case request and department assignment document for State v. Church. Confirmed by local 03-17-23 PDF and Master Evidence Index.

rows.title: Original criminal complaint filed (Reno Justice Court)
rows.summary: Reno Justice Court complaint charged Grand Larceny of a Motor Vehicle. DDA Dianne S. Drinkwater identified on complaint per Master Evidence Index.

rows.title: Amended complaint, preliminary-hearing waiver, and bindover
rows.summary: First amended complaint added Unlawful Taking of a Motor Vehicle. Waiver of preliminary examination entered. Bindover/Justice Court materials transferred matter to district court.

rows.title: Pretrial services court note — release condition issues
rows.summary: Pretrial court note documented release-condition issues and a failure-to-appear warrant per Case Exhibits spreadsheet and local 09-01-23 court-note files.

rows.title: District Court Information filed; second preliminary-examination waiver
rows.summary: State filed District Court Information in CR23-0657. Second preliminary-examination waiver appears in record set.

rows.title: Arraignment — not guilty plea; 60-day trial rule waived
rows.summary: Case exhibit data states Cameron Church pleaded not guilty, waived the 60-day trial rule, and had Cooper Brinson as appointed counsel.

rows.title: Own-recognizance release revoked
rows.summary: Order Revoking Own Recognizance entered. Pretrial Services recommendation modified supervision after new arrest on October 8, 2023.

rows.title: Substitution of counsel — Sydney Hutt replaces Cooper Brinson
rows.summary: Sydney Hutt replaced Cooper Brinson as defense counsel within the Alternate Public Defender's Office.

rows.title: Trial continued — stand-in counsel requests vacation of trial date
rows.summary: Court minutes summarized in exhibit spreadsheet state stand-in counsel moved to vacate trial date due to lead counsel's emergency leave.

rows.title: Pro se motion to dismiss and request for new counsel filed
rows.summary: Cameron Church filed pro se motion alleging ineffective assistance, lack of victim, and issues with a Young hearing request.

rows.title: Order striking defendant's pro se motion to dismiss
rows.summary: Judge Barry L. Breslow struck defendant's pro se motion because Church was represented by counsel. Issue: hybrid representation / Faretta / access to courts.

rows.title: Sydney Hutt requests Young hearing
rows.summary: Sydney Hutt filed request for a Young hearing, formalizing claimed breakdown in attorney-client relationship.

rows.title: Young hearing — APD relieved; conflict counsel referral ordered
rows.summary: Young-hearing minutes/summary state APD office was relieved due to conflict and matter was referred for conflict-counsel appointment.

rows.title: Galen Carrico appointed as conflict counsel
rows.summary: Recommendation and order appointing counsel identifies Galen Carrico as appointed conflict counsel in CR23-0657.

rows.title: Jury trial set for March 24, 2025
rows.summary: Application for setting placed jury trial on March 24, 2025 and pretrial motions hearing on February 13, 2025.

rows.title: Second Young motion filed by Carrico
rows.summary: Galen Carrico filed second Young motion citing breakdown in communication. Defendant materials allege counsel disregarded instructions.

rows.title: Order granting motion to strike pro se sanctions filing
rows.summary: Court struck the sanctions motion and response under hybrid-representation rules.

rows.title: Pro se motion for leave to file pro se motions
rows.summary: Church filed pro se motion seeking leave to file pro se motions because of alleged counsel ineffectiveness and obstruction.

rows.title: Pro se motion to dismiss indictment with prejudice
rows.summary: Church filed motion alleging systemic constitutional violations, prosecutorial misconduct, and collusion. Issue flags: speedy trial, IAC, prosecutorial misconduct (allegations in filing).

rows.title: Pro se sanctions motion against Chief DDA Mary Kandaras
rows.summary: Church filed pro se sanctions motion alleging Chief DDA Kandaras intimidated him to cease direct communication. Allegations in filing.

rows.title: Order striking defendant's late-November pro se filings
rows.summary: Judge Breslow struck late-November pro se filings because defendant was represented by counsel.

rows.title: Young hearing — competency evaluation ordered; proceedings stayed
rows.summary: Transcript/minutes identify Judge Breslow, DDA Aziz Merchant, Galen Carrico, and Cameron Church. Court ordered competency evaluation, stayed proceedings, and restricted pro se filings.

rows.title: Competency order entered nunc pro tunc to December 5
rows.summary: Competency order entered nunc pro tunc to December 5. Church filed pro se mandamus petition challenging Judge Breslow and DA's office.

rows.title: Order to show cause — competency evaluation compliance
rows.summary: Court issued order to show cause for alleged non-compliance with competency evaluation and threatened bail/release consequences.

rows.title: OSC hearing — pretrial release revoked; no-bail bench warrant issued
rows.summary: Minutes state pretrial release was revoked and no-bail bench warrant issued for failure to appear.

rows.title: Bench warrant and extradition order materials
rows.summary: Record set includes bench warrant/no-bail hold and extradition order materials dated December 19, 2024.

rows.title: State files motion for contempt over pro se filings
rows.summary: State filed motion for contempt seeking jail time for pro se filings. Exhibit spreadsheet also links this date to initiation of 42 U.S.C. Section 1983 federal action.

rows.title: Trial and pretrial motions hearing vacated pending competency
rows.summary: Order vacated March 2025 trial and pretrial motions hearing pending competency proceedings.

rows.title: State moves for no-bail hold
rows.summary: State moved for no-bail hold citing harassment patterns and noncompliance with orders per exhibit spreadsheet. State allegations.

rows.title: Warrant service
rows.summary: Warrant-service document dated April 23, 2025 in record set.

rows.title: Competency court order — transfer to Department 7
rows.summary: Nunc pro tunc/order-for-competency record transferred case to Department 7 / competency court.

rows.title: First competency report — Lindsay Coyle
rows.summary: Forensic competency report by Lindsay Coyle filed in CR23-0657. Contains medical/mental-health information; redaction required before public posting.

rows.title: Second competency report — Dr. Rachael Pinkerman
rows.summary: Confidential competency evaluation by Dr. Rachael Pinkerman. Exhibit index states evaluator found competency under Dusky standard and noted refusal to participate due to retaliation. Redaction required.

rows.title: Parties stipulate to third competency evaluation
rows.summary: Parties stipulated to third competency evaluation due to conflicting opinions from first two evaluators.

rows.title: Defense counsel Samuel Figueroa moves to withdraw
rows.summary: Samuel Figueroa moved to withdraw citing relationship breakdown and defendant's desire for self-representation or motions counsel considered repugnant.

rows.title: Court orders third competency evaluation
rows.summary: Court ordered third competency evaluation.

rows.title: Third competency report — Lisa Foerster
rows.summary: Forensic report by Lisa Foerster. Exhibit index states report found competence and described sophisticated understanding of legal process. Redaction required.

rows.title: Competency court remand — defendant found competent
rows.summary: Record set includes proposed/order materials finding competence and remanding case from competency court.

rows.title: Substitution of attorney — Joel Merchant enters as counsel
rows.summary: Substitution of attorney relieved prior counsel and substituted Joel Merchant as counsel of record per exhibit spreadsheet.

rows.title: Status hearing — supervised bail reinstated; pro se restrictions continued
rows.summary: Minutes report supervised bail at $25,000, continued restrictions on pro se filings, and no-contact directions involving DA's office.

rows.title: Petition for writ of mandamus — recusal of Judge Breslow sought
rows.summary: Church filed petition for writ of mandamus seeking recusal of Judge Breslow and venue transfer.

rows.title: Order striking writ — defendant represented by counsel
rows.summary: Court struck writ because defendant was represented by counsel.

rows.title: Figueroa withdrawal granted; no-bail warrant issued; Faretta conditioned on arrest
rows.summary: Minutes state Figueroa withdrawal was granted, no-bail warrant issued for failure to appear, and Faretta hearing was conditioned on arrest.

rows.title: State files notice of bail-condition violation
rows.summary: DDA Stege filed notice alleging email to DDA Merchant regarding a medical emergency constituted a threat. State allegation.

rows.title: Defendant files notice of constitutional violations — 900+ days of delay alleged
rows.summary: Church filed notice/demand for clarification charting 900+ days of delay and claimed hearing defects. Allegations in filing.

rows.title: Renewed motion for criminal referral of DDA Aziz Merchant
rows.summary: Church filed renewed referral motion alleging fraud on the court regarding amended complaints and GPS tracker representations. Allegations in filing.

rows.title: Motion to dismiss for structural constitutional violations; warrants quashed; written findings demanded
rows.summary: Church filed motion and declaration seeking dismissal, warrant quashal, and written constitutional findings. Challenges nunc pro tunc alterations and competency-stay issues. Allegations in filing.
